relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After the uproar, Paul encourages the disciples and moves toward Macedonia.
Many words strengthen the regions.
A plot redirects his route through Macedonia.
A multi-region team accompanies the mission.
The companions wait in Troas.
After Unleavened Bread, the journey reaches Troas for seven days.
Bread-breaking and word continue until midnight.
Many lamps shine in the upper room.
Eutychus falls from sleep and height into death.
Paul embraces him and announces life still in him.
Bread, conversation, and daybreak return after the fall.
The child is brought alive, and comfort overflows.
The team sails while Paul goes by land.
Paul rejoins them at Assos.
The voyage moves island by island toward Miletus.
Paul hurries toward Jerusalem for Pentecost.
From Miletus he summons Ephesian elders.
He recalls his whole way of being among them.
Service, humility, tears, and trials marked his ministry.
Nothing profitable was held back publicly or house to house.
His witness was turning toward the Divine and trust toward Jesus.
Bound in Spirit, he goes to Jerusalem without knowing details.
Holy Spirit testifies that bonds and pressures await.
His life is poured toward completing the course and witnessing grace.
He knows they will not see his face again.
He bears witness to clean responsibility.
The whole counsel of the Divine was not withheld.
The elders must guard themselves and shepherd the Spirit-set flock.
Heavy wolves will threaten the flock.
Twisted speech can arise even from within.
Wakefulness remembers three years of tearful admonition.
They are entrusted to the Divine and the word of grace.
Paul coveted no one’s wealth.
His own hands served his needs and companions.
Labor becomes help for the weak; giving is more blessed than receiving.
He kneels and prays with them all.
Weeping and embrace fill the farewell.
The grief centers on not seeing his face again as they escort him to the ship.
